title: Listening - Part 3: A Day Out with Friends
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a day out they spent with friends. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H what each speaker enjoyed most about the day. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a day out they spent with friends. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H what each speaker enjoyed most about the day. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about a day out with friends.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: My friends originally suggested going to a popular beach, but on a hot weekend it would have been packed. One of them knew a path to a small lake reached by walking through woodland. [19]When we arrived, there were no shops, music or groups competing for space, just the four of us sitting by the water with hardly another person in sight.[/19] We didn't do anything exciting, but escaping the noise and crowds for a whole afternoon was exactly what I needed after a busy week.||Speaker 2 Man: We went to an outdoor activity centre for my friend's birthday. I thought we would spend the afternoon playing simple games, but she had booked a high ropes course in the trees. Heights aren't my favourite thing. [20]The best moment was finally completing a difficult crossing above the ground while my friends encouraged me from the next platform; I couldn't believe I'd managed it.[/20] Afterwards we had cake, which was pleasant, but overcoming that challenge is the part of the day I keep remembering.||Speaker 3 Woman: Instead of going to a restaurant, we cycled to a picnic site beside a river. Everyone was asked to bring one ingredient, but nobody knew exactly what the others were bringing. It could have gone badly. [21]We ended up making an enormous salad and warm sandwiches together on a little camping stove, and sitting down to eat something we'd all contributed to was surprisingly enjoyable.[/21] The ride home was tiring because we'd eaten too much, but the meal made the day feel more special than buying food somewhere.||Speaker 4 Man: I grew up near the canal but had never travelled on it, because I assumed boat trips were aimed at tourists. A friend found a company hiring small boats for the day and convinced us to try. [22]I loved moving slowly through familiar parts of town from the water, opening the locks ourselves and realising how different everything looked from a boat.[/22] We got rather wet in a shower, and I wouldn't choose it in winter, but it was a completely new way to spend time locally.||Speaker 5 Woman: My friends and I spent Sunday at a huge second-hand market. I enjoy browsing, but I had a particular reason for agreeing to go: I'd been trying to find an old edition of a children's book my mother once owned. [23]After looking through countless boxes, I spotted exactly the same cover on a stall and bought it for far less than I expected.[/23] We also had good street food and laughed at some strange objects, but finally finding that book made the trip unforgettable for me.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: My friends originally suggested going to a popular beach, but on a hot weekend it would have been packed. One of them knew a path to a small lake reached by walking through woodland. When we arrived, there were no shops, music or groups competing for space, just the four of us sitting by the water with hardly another person in sight. We didn't do anything exciting, but escaping the noise and crowds for a whole afternoon was exactly what I needed after a busy week.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: We went to an outdoor activity centre for my friend's birthday. I thought we would spend the afternoon playing simple games, but she had booked a high ropes course in the trees. Heights aren't my favourite thing. The best moment was finally completing a difficult crossing above the ground while my friends encouraged me from the next platform; I couldn't believe I'd managed it. Afterwards we had cake, which was pleasant, but overcoming that challenge is the part of the day I keep remembering.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: Instead of going to a restaurant, we cycled to a picnic site beside a river. Everyone was asked to bring one ingredient, but nobody knew exactly what the others were bringing. It could have gone badly. We ended up making an enormous salad and warm sandwiches together on a little camping stove, and sitting down to eat something we'd all contributed to was surprisingly enjoyable. The ride home was tiring because we'd eaten too much, but the meal made the day feel more special than buying food somewhere.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: I grew up near the canal but had never travelled on it, because I assumed boat trips were aimed at tourists. A friend found a company hiring small boats for the day and convinced us to try. I loved moving slowly through familiar parts of town from the water, opening the locks ourselves and realising how different everything looked from a boat. We got rather wet in a shower, and I wouldn't choose it in winter, but it was a completely new way to spend time locally.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: My friends and I spent Sunday at a huge second-hand market. I enjoy browsing, but I had a particular reason for agreeing to go: I'd been trying to find an old edition of a children's book my mother once owned. After looking through countless boxes, I spotted exactly the same cover on a stall and bought it for far less than I expected. We also had good street food and laughed at some strange objects, but finally finding that book made the trip unforgettable for me.

options.text: taking part in a physical challenge
finding something they had been looking for
trying an unfamiliar form of transport

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker particularly appreciated spending time in a peaceful location without crowds (evidence [19]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The speaker's highlight was succeeding on a difficult high ropes activity (evidence [20]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The speaker valued preparing and eating a shared meal with friends (evidence [21]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The speaker most enjoyed experiencing local surroundings from a small canal boat (evidence [22]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speaker was pleased to discover a particular old book that she had been seeking (evidence [23]).
